config_backup.export_desc: Download every operator-tunable setting (incl. hosts, host_groups, schedules, notify_templates, tuning_* overrides, AI memory) as a single human-readable JSON file. Commit it to a private git repo for change tracking, or keep it as a one-file disaster-recovery seed for a fresh deploy.
config_backup.secret_note: Secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens, private keys) are redacted to the literal string "__OMITTED__". On import, redacted entries are SKIPPED — your live secrets are preserved. Safe to commit the export to a private git repo.
config_backup.import_desc: Upload a previously-exported JSON file. Settings are upserted per-key; schedules and AI memory are REPLACED entirely. Operator confirms before anything writes.
config_backup.import_warning: ⚠ Import OVERWRITES the schedules table and the AI memory table in full. Settings are merged per-key. Take a fresh export FIRST if you want a rollback point.
config_backup.saved_desc: Files written by the `config_backup` schedule kind (or the manual "Save to disk now" button above). Download / restore / delete from here.
config_backup.saved_empty: No saved snapshots yet. Use "Save to disk now" or schedule a `config_backup` job in Admin → Schedules.
config_backup.import_confirm_body:
{name}

Settings will be merged per-key. Schedules and AI memory will be REPLACED entirely. Redacted secret entries are skipped — live secret values are preserved.
config_backup.delete_confirm_body:
{name}

The file will be removed from the data volume. This can't be undone.

drawer.external_warning: ⚠ This service has no associated Portainer stack (external / CLI-deployed).
drawer.retag_latest_title: Rewrite the stack's compose file to track :latest instead of the current pinned tag, then redeploy. The original tag stays in the registry — manual rollback still works.
drawer.retag_tag_title: Switch this image to a different tag (e.g. :latest, :2, :v2-stable). Mutates the compose file (stack-managed) or recreates the container (standalone) to track the new tag. The original tag stays in the registry — manual rollback still works.
drawer.retag_tag_help: Type the new tag (e.g. latest, 2, v2-stable). Press Enter to apply, Esc to cancel.
drawer.task_error_resolved_label: Recent task failures (resolved — service is healthy)
drawer.task_error_known_issue_vxlan:
Known Docker Swarm overlay-network bug — the kernel kept a leftover vx-NNNNNN-XXXX VXLAN interface from a previous task and the new task can't recreate it. Fix on the affected node:
1) docker network ls to find the overlay network id
2) docker network rm <network_id> if no other service is using it (Swarm will recreate when the next task starts), OR
3) on the node where the task is failing: sudo ip link delete vx-XXXXXX-YYYY for each leftover interface (ip -d link show type vxlan lists them), then redeploy the service
4) Last resort: sudo systemctl restart docker on the affected node — clears all leftover interfaces but disrupts every running container on that node. Run this from Admin → SSH if SSH is configured for the host.
drawer.task_error_known_issue_image_pull: The image couldn't be pulled. Common causes: registry credentials missing or expired, the tag doesn't exist, Docker Hub rate-limit hit (use authenticated pulls or a mirror), or a transient registry outage. Verify docker pull <image> works manually on the manager; if it fails the same way, fix the registry config in OmniGrid's Admin → Portainer or set DOCKERHUB_USER/DOCKERHUB_TOKEN in .env.
drawer.task_error_known_issue_mount: A bind-mount or volume the service expects is missing on the node where the task scheduled. Check that the mount source path exists on the node, that NFS/SMB shares are mounted, and that filesystem permissions allow Docker to access them. For host-path binds, the path must exist BEFORE the service starts (Docker won't create it).
drawer.task_error_known_issue_placement: No node satisfies the service's placement constraints. Common causes: a constraint references a label that no node has, a node.role==manager constraint with no manager available, or the constraint syntax is wrong. Inspect with docker service inspect <svc> --format '{{.Spec.TaskTemplate.Placement.Constraints}}' and docker node ls --format '{{.Hostname}} {{.Status.State}} {{.ManagerStatus.Reachability}}'.
drawer.task_error_known_issue_resources: The node ran out of memory, CPU, or disk for the service's reservations. Either lower the service's resource reservations (docker service update --reserve-memory 0 <svc>), free up resources on the node, or add another node to the swarm to spread the load.
drawer.task_error_action_force_restart_help: Issues a force-update on this service. Sometimes the kernel cleans up the stale VXLAN interface between task attempts; safe first try.
drawer.task_error_action_ssh_fix_help: Opens an SSH terminal on the failing node so you can run ip -d link show type vxlan to identify the leftover interface, then sudo ip link delete vx-XXXXXX-YYYY to remove it. As a last resort sudo systemctl restart docker clears all leftover interfaces but disrupts every running container on that node.
drawer.task_error_restart_queued: Force-restart queued. Watch the row for the new task to come up; the error should clear automatically when it does.
drawer.task_error_ssh_no_host: No curated host matches "{node}" — add it under Admin → Hosts (or set its `id` to the node's hostname) to enable the SSH-fix button.
drawer.task_error_action_cleanup_overlay_help: Uses the Portainer API to find the overlay network matching the failing subnet, verifies no other containers are using it, removes it, then force-updates the service. Docker recreates the overlay + a fresh VXLAN interface when the new task lands. SSH-free; safe for single-stack overlay networks (the typical compose-deploy pattern). Aborts cleanly when the network is shared with another stack.
drawer.task_error_cleanup_overlay_done: Overlay {network} removed and service force-updated. Watch for the new task to come up.

swarm_agent_banner.title: Portainer agent unreachable on {count} Swarm node(s)
swarm_agent_banner.body: Swarm tasks claim containers are running on these nodes, but every stats call has failed for the last few gather cycles. The most likely cause is that the Portainer agent on these nodes failed to re-register after a manager restart. Force-restart the agent service on the manager to clear it:
swarm_agent_banner.row: {host} — agent unreachable for {fails} consecutive gather(s); {cids} container(s) affected
swarm_agent_banner.restart_help: Auto-discovers the Portainer agent service and force-updates it. Op shows in the queue + history.
swarm_agent_banner.restart_queued: Agent restart queued — see ops queue / history for progress
swarm_agent_banner.confirm_text: This will force-update the Portainer agent global service. Every node's agent task respawns and re-registers with the manager. Workload services are not touched. The agent service is auto-discovered (by image prefix + name pattern); if multiple candidates are found the op fails with a list so you can pick manually.

metrics.disk_io_idle: No disk I/O activity in this window
metrics.source_temperature: Per-sensor thermal readings (°C). Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.t — a {sensor: celsius} dict). Hosts whose agent doesn't expose any thermal sensor (most VMs, some hardware lacking a hwmon driver) hide this chart entirely. Chart line is the peak across all sensors at each tick; the live header lists every sensor with its current reading.
metrics.source_cpu: CPU usage (0–100%). Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.cpu) when configured. The node-exporter sampler doesn't track CPU yet, so NE-only hosts show no data here.
metrics.source_memory: Memory usage. Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.mp / mu) for Beszel-monitored hosts; node-exporter (host_mem_used / host_mem_total from host_metrics_samples) for NE-only hosts.
metrics.source_disk: Root-filesystem usage. Source: Beszel (system_stats.dp) or node-exporter (host_disk_used / host_disk_total).
metrics.source_network: Per-NIC throughput, excluding loopback / Docker bridges / veth pairs. Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.nr / .ns) when the agent has NICS=eth0 configured. Falls back to node-exporter rates from host_net_samples when Beszel returns zeros AND the host has ne_url set. Pure node-exporter for NE-only hosts.
metrics.source_disk_io: Block-device read/write rates. Source: Beszel (system_stats.dr / .dw) or node-exporter (host_disk_read_bps / host_disk_write_bps from host_metrics_samples). Excludes loop / ram / fd / sr / zram devices; keeps dm-* / md* (NAS volumes).
metrics.source_load_avg: 1m / 5m / 15m load averages. Source: Beszel agent only (system_stats.la). Not surfaced by the node-exporter sampler today.
metrics.source_bandwidth: Combined bandwidth (rx + tx). Same source as Network — Beszel or node-exporter.
metrics.source_swap: Swap usage % and used GiB. Source: Beszel agent only (system_stats.s / .su). Not surfaced by the node-exporter sampler today.
metrics.ping_loss_title: Packet loss across the polled window
metrics.source_ping: ICMP round-trip latency in milliseconds. Source: OmniGrid's built-in ping sampler (host_ping_samples), opt-in per host via Admin → Hosts → ping_enabled. Hosts without the toggle hide this chart entirely.
metrics.source_snmp_cpu: CPU usage from the SNMP probe (hrProcessorLoad table average). Source: SNMP only. Surfaces on hosts where the SNMP agent exposes the host-resources MIB; missing on devices that ship a stripped MIB profile.
metrics.source_snmp_load: Load averages derived from the SNMP probe (UCD-SNMP-MIB::laLoad). Source: SNMP only — UCD-flavoured agents (Linux net-snmp, OPNsense, OpenWRT). Devices without the UCD MIB hide this chart.
metrics.source_snmp_memory: Memory usage from the SNMP probe (hrStorageRam entries in the host-resources MIB). Source: SNMP only.
metrics.source_snmp_throughput: Per-interface throughput from the SNMP probe (ifHCInOctets / ifHCOutOctets, falling back to the 32-bit ifInOctets / ifOutOctets counters when high-capacity counters aren't published). Source: SNMP only.
metrics.source_gpu: GPU utilisation. Source: Beszel agent (system_stats.g) when the host has an NVIDIA / AMD GPU exposed to the agent. Hosts without GPU readings hide this chart entirely.
metrics.source_dell_temps: Per-probe temperature readings (°C) from the iDRAC's temperatureProbeTable walk (1.3.6.1.4.1.674.10892.5.4.700.20). Source: SNMP only — Inlet / Exhaust / CPU1 / CPU2 + any chipset / DIMM / disk-bay probes the chassis exposes. The sampler writes one row per probe per tick into host_snmp_temp_samples (skip-don't-synthesize: missing reads are dropped, never stored as 0). Chart needs at least 2 ticks (~10 minutes at the default 300s interval) before lines appear; until then 'collecting data' renders.

admin_hosts.heading: Hosts
admin_hosts.intro: Define the machines you want to monitor and map each to the names used by every enabled provider. OmniGrid fetches all providers on every refresh and merges the best values per host — missing fields degrade to what the other providers returned.
admin_hosts.snmp_walk_concurrency_hint: How many SNMP walks run in parallel against THIS host. Blank = the global default (1, the safety floor). Server-class BMCs like Dell iDRAC, Cisco IMC, and Supermicro IPMI handle parallel queries fine and need 4-8 to fit pysnmp's per-walk overhead inside the probe budget. Leave blank for low-power printers / NAS / OpenWrt-class gear that drop UDP packets at higher concurrency.
admin_hosts.snmp_wall_clock_budget_hint: Total wall-clock budget for ONE SNMP probe against THIS host (seconds). Blank = the global default (60s). Slow iDRAC chassis with the BIOS / virtual-disk / power-supply walks may need 90-120s; embedded snmpd's typically finish well under the default. Range 5..600s.
admin_hosts.snmp_vendors_hint: Which vendor MIBs to walk for THIS host. Leave every box unchecked to auto-detect from sysDescr (covers 95% of agents). Check explicit vendors to bypass auto-detect — useful when sysDescr is stripped, novel hardware, or to force vendor walks the auto-detect would skip. Pruning irrelevant vendor walks drops a Dell iDRAC's 67-walk probe to ~50 walks (~13s saved at concurrency=1).
admin_hosts.snmp_vendors_explicit: Override: walking only base + {vendors}. Auto-detect bypassed.
admin_hosts.snmp_exclude_mounts_hint: Mount paths to drop from the SNMP storage extractor's output for THIS host (one path per line, or comma-separated). Some agents mis-classify pseudo-filesystems as fixed disks — DD-WRT in particular reports `/opt` as a 232 GB hrStorageFixedDisk on a 16 MB router. Universal pseudo-fs paths (/dev, /proc, /sys, /run, /var/run, /var/lock, /dev/shm) are filtered automatically; this list is for device-specific phantoms.
admin_hosts.snmp_exclude_mounts_help: One mount path per line. Match is exact or prefix-with-slash (e.g. `/opt` matches both `/opt` and `/opt/foo`). Max 32 entries.

groups.heading: Host groups
groups.intro: Collect hosts into collapsible sections in the Hosts view by matching their Custom # against a numeric range. Example: "ISP Routers" covers 1–4, "Gateways" covers 5–8. Hosts that don't fall into any range land in the "Ungrouped" bucket at the bottom.
groups.invalid_range: Invalid range for group "{name}" — end must be ≥ start.
groups.overlap: Groups "{a_name}" ({a_start}–{a_end}) and "{b_name}" ({b_start}–{b_end}) overlap. Ranges must be disjoint.
groups.empty: No groups yet. Add one below to start bucketing hosts by Custom #.
groups.nesting_hint: Nesting is 2 levels deep. A sub-group's range must fit inside its parent's range, and must not overlap any non-parent group.
groups.range_required: Range start and end are required.
groups.err_self_parent: A group cannot be its own parent.
groups.err_parent_missing: Parent "{name}" doesn't exist — type the exact name of a top-level group.
groups.err_parent_is_sub: Parent "{name}" is itself a sub-group. Nesting is limited to 2 levels.
groups.err_not_contained: Sub-group range must fit inside parent "{parent}" ({ps}–{pe}).
groups.err_overlap: Range overlaps "{other}" ({os}–{oe}). Ranges must be disjoint except for parent↔sub-group pairs.
groups.number_hint: Optional display prefix shown before the group name in the Hosts view (e.g. "32 Smart & IOT Routers"). Must be unique across all groups when set.
groups.invalid_number: Number must be a positive integer.
groups.err_number_dupe: Number already used by "{other}". Group numbers must be unique.
groups.ssh_clear_title: Erase the stored password on save
groups.ssh_hint: Resolution order: per-host override → this sub-group / parent → global default. Most-specific value wins.
groups.children_toggle_title: Collapse this parent group's sub-rows so the editor stays compact while you add more items.
groups.parent_toggle_title: Collapse this parent group's edit form. The summary row (number / name / range) stays visible so you can still identify the group at a glance.
groups.collapse_all_parents_title: Hide every top-level group's edit form. The summary row stays visible for each so you can still identify them.
groups.expand_all_parents_title: Re-show every top-level group's full edit form.
groups.collapse_all_title: Hide every parent group's sub-rows so only top-level rows are visible.
groups.preview_title: Preview — how this group will render in the Hosts view.
groups.add_subgroup_title: Add a sub-group nested under "{parent}". Range pre-fills inside the parent's range so you don't trip the containment validator.

admin_assets.title: Asset Inventory
admin_assets.tunables_intro: Outbound HTTP wall-clocks for the asset-inventory API. The token-probe handshake is a lightweight one-shot call; the asset fetch is a paginated read across every known asset record. Slow upstream / tunneled deploys may need longer caps; tight-watchdog deploys can lower. All edits commit through the Asset Inventory Save button.
admin_assets.intro: Pull an asset catalogue (vendor / model / serial / location) from your asset-management upstream. Pick one of two auth modes below. Manually refreshed — the cache at /app/data/asset_inventory.json is the source of truth the host drawer reads from.
admin_assets.auth_mode_oauth2_help: Client_credentials grant against a token endpoint; OmniGrid exchanges your web-service user + password for a short-lived bearer token on every refresh.
admin_assets.auth_mode_lifetime_help: A single static key minted once (OAuth2 with lifetime=1) and pasted in below. OmniGrid POSTs to {base_url}/services.php with X-Authorization: Bearer <key> — no token exchange at refresh time.
admin_assets.clear_secret_text: Removes the stored password. Refresh will fail until a new one is saved.
admin_assets.clear_lifetime_token_text: Removes the stored lifetime token. Refresh will fail until a new one is saved.
admin_assets.service_action_help: Two form parameters services.php routes on. For the asset catalogue, upstream expects service=assets and action=get_assets_custom_number_range — ensure the token has the assets:read scope.
admin_assets.range_help: Range bounds for actions like get_assets_custom_number_range. When both are set and action matches, OmniGrid paginates automatically in 50-batches (the server's default cap). Leave blank for actions that don't use a range.
admin_assets.edit_url_help: Prefix used by the host drawer's "Edit on upstream" link. The asset id is appended at the end (e.g. "…&si=42"). Placeholders {id} / {custom_number} / {base} are also supported when the URL needs the id mid-path. Leave blank to hide the edit link.
admin_assets.verify_tls_label: Verify TLS certificate (uncheck for self-signed asset API endpoints)
admin_assets.save_requires_test: Run a successful Test connection before saving.
admin_assets.cache_empty: No cached snapshot. Click Refresh now to populate.

admin_port_scan.title: Port Scan
admin_port_scan.intro: On-demand TCP-connect port scanner. Unlike the host-stats providers (Beszel / Pulse / node-exporter / Webmin / SNMP / Ping) port scan never polls in the background — it runs ONLY when you click "Scan ports" in a host drawer or ask the AI palette to scan. Per-host overrides (enabled / ports / timeout / concurrency) live in Admin → Hosts under each row's Port Scan section.
admin_port_scan.udp_master_toggle_help: Run UDP probes alongside the TCP scan when this is on. UDP uses per-protocol templates (DNS 53, DHCP 67, NTP 123, SNMP 161, mDNS 5353, etc.) so well-known services reply with a real datagram instead of being marked open-filtered. Disabled by default — UDP traffic is more conspicuous on the network and some services log every probe. Requires the master Port-scan toggle.
admin_port_scan.udp_default_ports_hint: CSV / range syntax (e.g. "53,67,69,123,137,161,500,514,520,623,1900,5353"). When blank, the scanner uses a built-in list of common UDP services. Range 1..11000.
admin_port_scan.tcp_section_intro: TCP-connect scan defaults. Each port gets a real handshake — closed ports are indistinguishable from filtered. Default-ports CSV is the fallback when a host has no per-host override; the four tunables shape per-port timeout, parallel probe count, the outer wall-clock budget for a single scan, and the banner-grab read window when banner-grab is enabled.
admin_port_scan.udp_section_intro: UDP scan defaults (Stage 2). UDP probes run in parallel with TCP when enabled; closed ports are indistinguishable from open-filtered without per-protocol templates, so the scanner uses well-known protocol-specific payloads (DNS 53, DHCP 67, NTP 123, SNMP 161, mDNS 5353, etc.) where supported. Off by default — UDP traffic is more conspicuous on the network and triggers logged events on some services.
admin_port_scan.schedule_section_intro: Knobs for the `port_scan_refresh` schedule kind. Create a schedule from Admin → Schedules with kind `port_scan_refresh` and the cron-style cadence you want; these knobs only shape HOW MUCH work each fire does. Default cadence in the schedule editor is every 15 minutes; the three knobs below tune host-batch size, minimum age before re-scan, and per-host parallelism.

admin_ssh.title: SSH
admin_ssh.intro: Global SSH credentials OmniGrid uses to run one-shot commands on a host from its drawer. Admin-only, dry-run-first, audit-logged. Per-host overrides (user / port / disabled) live in Admin → Hosts under each row's SSH section.
admin_ssh.tunables_intro: Operator-tunable knobs for the SSH terminal — WS heartbeat cadence (keeps the websocket alive past reverse-proxy idle timers), TCP connect timeout (caps the initial socket handshake), login timeout (caps the post-connect auth handshake), and connection-close wait (caps how long `conn.wait_closed()` blocks at session teardown). All edits commit through the SSH section's Save button.
admin_ssh.clear_key_title: Erase the stored SSH private key (and its passphrase). Cannot be undone — paste a new key after to restore access.
admin_ssh.clear_passphrase_title: Erase the stored passphrase. The private key stays; the runner will try to parse it unprotected.
admin_ssh.clear_password_title: Erase the stored default password. Per-host password overrides still apply.
admin_ssh.password_help: Used when no private key is configured, or when a per-host row overrides with its own password. Key auth wins when both are set.
admin_ssh.fqdn_suffix_help: Appended to bare host IDs that don't already contain a dot. Normalised on save to include the leading dot (you can paste with or without).
admin_ssh.key_hint: An ed25519 or RSA private key in OpenSSH / PEM format. Blank on save keeps the current value. Paste a new value to replace.
admin_ssh.known_hosts_hint: Paste the target's known_hosts line(s) here to enable strict host-key verification. Leave blank to accept on first use (TOFU) — the fingerprint is always shown in the drawer so you can verify it out-of-band.
admin_ssh.custom_actions_hint: Named commands that appear as preset buttons in each host's SSH drawer. Use `{host}` in the command to substitute the target's resolved hostname at click time. Clicking a preset drops the rendered command into the textarea so you can preview before running.
admin_ssh.custom_actions_empty: No custom actions yet — add one below.
admin_ssh.destructive_hint: Regex patterns — one per line or comma-separated. Commands matching any of these require typed-hostname confirmation in the drawer. Blank falls back to the built-in list (rm / mkfs / dd / >/ / systemctl stop / reboot / poweroff / shutdown).
admin_ssh.test_widget_hint: Pick a curated host and run whoami with a short timeout. Uses the saved credentials above.

hosts_extra_ssh.disabled_not_up: SSH disabled — host is {status}. Bring the host online before running commands.
hosts_extra_ssh.confirm_prompt: Type the hostname ({host}) to confirm — this command matched a destructive pattern.
